name: node-quick-lru
title: node-quick-lru
description:
Simple "Least Recently Used" (LRU) cache
Useful when you need to cache something and limit memory usage.
Inspired by the hashlru algorithm, but instead uses Map to support
keys of any type, not just strings, and values can be undefined.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-quick-lru because Simple "Least Recently Used" (LRU) cache.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-quick-lru as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-quick-lru with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-quick-lru --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
